2606.22402.title: Reinforcement learning to improve large language model-based automated code compliance systems
2606.22402.paper_summary: Large language model (LLM)-based approaches for automated code compliance (ACC) of building regulations are prone to generating incorrect and hallucinated computer-processable rules. This paper introduces P4IR, a two-stage framework that uses supervised fine-tuning (SFT) to instill domain knowledge in an LLM, followed by Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) to improve the accuracy of the generated intermediate representations in the form of high-level code skeletons. The framework achieved reductions of up to 23.8% and 38.6% in tree edit distance and token-level Levenshtein distance respectively, relative to the SFT baselines. Comparative analysis demonstrates that this approach in a zero-shot setting outperforms leading LLMs in both code structure and semantics, specifically Claude Opus and Sonnet 4.5, GPT-5.2, Qwen-3-Max, and GLM-4.7, evaluated via few-shot prompting. Additionally, the GRPO stage produced a small yet statistically significant reduction in false positives. By combining SFT with GRPO to optimize directly for domain-specific objectives, this approach offers a path toward more accurate and reliable LLM-based ACC systems.
2606.22402.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLM）為基礎的自動化代碼合規性（ACC）方法容易產生不正確和虛構的計算機可處理規則。本文介紹了P4IR，一個兩階段框架，使用監督微調（SFT）來灌輸領域知識於LLM，然後通過群體相對政策優化（GRPO）來提高生成的中間表示的準確性，這些表示以高級代碼骨架的形式呈現。該框架在樹編輯距離和標記級Levenshtein距離方面分別實現了高達23.8%和38.6%的減少，相對於SFT基準。比較分析顯示，在零樣本設置中，這種方法在代碼結構和語義上超越了領先的LLM，特別是Claude Opus和Sonnet 4.5、GPT-5.2、Qwen-3-Max以及GLM-4.7，這些都是通過少量樣本提示進行評估的。此外，GRPO階段產生了小但統計上顯著的假陽性減少。通過將SFT與GRPO結合以直接優化特定領域的目標，這種方法為更準確和可靠的基於LLM的ACC系統提供了一條道路。
2606.22402.author: Jack Wei Lun Shi et.al.
2606.22402.authors: Jack Wei Lun Shi, Minghao Dang, Wawan Solihin, Leong Hien Poh, Justin K. W. Yeoh
